8774ee3b-19ab-45ca-8197-b68720c4b1b8_rev.product_description: Advice on pursuing higher education in the United States

actual_review_data.review_text:
As an international student who came to the US in 2008 and who benefited from the quality of education here, I have an advice for those who were thinking of coming to US for higher education any time soon (this year and 2021) - DO NOT come to the US for your higher education. There are 2 reasons for this that are straightforward:

(1) Quality of education on any online tool is subpar as compared to the on campus experience. Most classes are now being conducted online. I will be honest - it’s not worth paying the kind of money international students pay in tuition. There is no question that US has some of the best faculty but still the quality of online education would make you question what you are paying for. Several US higher education institutions today have impressive buildings and campuses, in many cases funded by higher tuition charged to international students. But, if you are not able to get any benefit from it, then what’s the point. 

(2) Several companies in US are not willing to hire international students with visa requirements. It’s likely there will be more visa restrictions imposed in this economy which will also limit your ability to find an internship or job. If you are paying upwards of 50000$ for a degree, you need to have a plan to pay that massive loan.
actual_review_data.product_description: Advice on pursuing higher education in the United States

initial_prediction_data.review_text:
As someone who has navigated the complexities of higher education in the United States, I want to share some insights that might help those considering this path. Pursuing a degree abroad can be both exciting and daunting, but with the right approach, it can open doors to incredible opportunities. 

First and foremost, don't underestimate the power of networking. Connect with alumni from your desired programs, attend webinars, and engage in online forums. These connections can provide invaluable advice and even lead to mentorship opportunities. Remember, your journey is not just about the degree; it's about the relationships you build along the way.

Additionally, resilience is key. There will be challenges, from adapting to a new culture to managing academic pressures. Embrace these experiences as part of your growth. Each obstacle you overcome will strengthen your adaptability and prepare you for future challenges.

Lastly, consider the practical applications of your education. How will it impact your career? Look for programs that offer hands-on experiences, internships, or projects that align with your career goals. This will enhance your learning and make you more competitive in the job market.

As we continue to navigate the evolving landscape of education and technology, let's support one another in our journeys. If you have questions or need guidance, feel free to reach out. We're in this together! #HigherEducation #Networking #Resilience #CareerGrowth

actual_review_data.review_text:
If you are interested in Data Engineering and you are based on LATAM, please read this!

At Factored we are on a mission to make a positive contribution to LATAM. The way we want to do this is by building high-caliber data teams ready to show the world the talent we have in Latin America.

Last year I embarked on a mission to help people understand more about Data Engineering. I had the opportunity to talk to almost 30 people about data engineering and share some insights about the profession with them.

I've decided again to open some slots on my schedule this year! :)
So, if you are into Data Engineering and you are interested to join Factored or you want to get some guidance on Data Engineering, please reach out to me. Also, if you know anyone interested in this topic, please tag them so we can get in touch! :)

You can find the link to grab a slot on my calendar in the comments :)
#factored #dataengineering #data #latinamerica

ab1fb966-d14e-4639-b504-00f7b6f9084b_rev.product_description: Exploration of personal challenges impacting careers

actual_review_data.review_text:
Estatisticas mundias estimam que "aprox. 70% das pessoas irão vivenciar sindrome de impostor pelo menos uma vez na vida" ( Essa experiência afeta principalmente as mulheres e, caso não identificada e persistente, pode ter impacto considerável em nossa carreira. 
A comunidade Brasil do The WIT Network faz um convite a você para participar de um bate papo online sobre o tema. Venha conhecer mais sobre a Sindrome de Impostor, bem como ouvir as estorias e dicas de profissionais talentosos que vivenciaram e superaram essa experiencia. 
Data: 16 de Outubro, às 19h BRT. 

[English]
World statistics estimate that "around 70% of people in the world will experience Imposter Syndrome at least once in their lives"( It is likely to affect women. 
Non identified cases or persistently experiencing Imposter Syndrome may affect your career progression. With this in mind, the Brazilian Community of The WIT Network invites you to join an online roundtable about the subject. Join us to know more about Imposter Syndrome and hear the amazing stories from talented professionals that overcame it.
Date: October 16th at 7PM BRT (GMT-3) 
Note: the event will be held in Portuguese.

initial_prediction_data.review_text:
As I reflect on my journey in the tech industry, I realize that personal challenges often shape our careers in profound ways. Navigating through tough times has taught me the importance of resilience and adaptability—qualities that are essential not just for personal growth, but also for fostering a supportive community around us. 

In our fast-paced world, it's easy to overlook the human side of our professional lives. Yet, sharing our struggles can create powerful connections and inspire others to embrace their own challenges. I encourage everyone in our community to open up about their experiences, whether it's overcoming obstacles in your career or finding the strength to pivot in a new direction. 

Let’s continue to support one another, share our stories, and build a network that thrives on empathy and collective growth. Remember, it's not just about the achievements we celebrate, but also the journeys we undertake together. #CommunitySupport #Resilience #PersonalGrowth #TechJourney

Now, it is time to start a new journey full of new innovation challenges. 
 Let's empower every people to innovate.
#newjourney #gratefulness #innovation

actual_review_data.review_text:
I rarely reshare content, but this one deeply resonated.

Simon Sinek nails what it means to truly lead. He also nails what it means to truly love.

It's a rare thing that someone can point out something so obviously true but yet so fleeting that you immediately wanna say “This makes so much sense how did I not see this”.

This video also made me very aware of all the little things I am doing, I am not doing and I should be doing. Especially concerning showing appreciation for certain people. 

This video made me reach out to a person from a very vulnerable place and it rekindled a deep connection.

What are some small ways in which you can better show your love / leadership (pick one)?

initial_prediction_data.review_text:
As I reflect on my journey as a Senior Software Engineer at Microsoft, I’m reminded of the profound impact that leadership and relationship-building have on our personal and professional growth. Navigating the tech landscape can often feel overwhelming, but it’s the connections we foster that truly make a difference. 

I’ve learned that effective leadership isn’t just about guiding a team; it’s about creating an environment where everyone feels valued and empowered to share their ideas. This is especially important in our rapidly evolving industry, where collaboration drives innovation.

One of the most rewarding aspects of my role has been mentoring aspiring engineers, particularly women in tech. I’ve seen firsthand how a supportive network can transform careers and inspire confidence. It’s a reminder that we all have the power to uplift others and contribute to a more inclusive tech community.

As we continue to embrace challenges and celebrate our wins, let’s prioritize building meaningful relationships. Whether it’s through mentorship, collaboration, or simply sharing our experiences, we can create a culture of support that benefits us all. 

Here’s to resilience, creativity, and the incredible connections we build along the way! #WomenInTech #Leadership #Mentorship #CommunitySupport

actual_review_data.review_text:
Good Day Friends, 
I would like to share my understanding of "COGS Split" in SAP S/4 HANA 1809 in a quick presentation.

This concept has been since 'SFin 1.0' as its one of much requested features from SAP customers.

Refer Note 2399030 (Splitting the costs of goods sold in S/4HANA)

Hope you will find it useful to some extent. Thank you. 

 #sapfico #hana #COGSSplit #S/4HANAFinance1809 #sap

I strongly recommend you do the below:

#AGALAL #Share_Knowledge

dc0b5d74-87d9-4fe3-8883-aef9d0924114_rev.product_description: Centralized customer payments in ERP systems

actual_review_data.review_text:
Centralized Customer Payments for Headquarters and Subsidiaries by Komathi T SAP Labs India 

Welcome to the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Two-tier ERP blog series. 

In this blog, let’s look at centralized customer payments in a Two-tier deployment model.

S/4HANA customer payments is a public cloud solution delivered in the SAP Cloud Platform, which provides a self-service offering to enable our customers to deploy electronic bill presentment and payment processes. In this Two-tier scenario, where headquarters is running On-Premise (ECC6+ or S/4HANA On-premise) and the subsidiary is running on S/4HANA cloud, SAP S/4HANA cloud for customer payment serves as a centralized customer payment solution for both headquarter as well as subsidiary.

While running in such two-tier landscape, processing repetitive customer inquiries regarding invoice copies, underpayment, dispute cases, matching incoming payments with open items because of Time zone issues, Language barriers, Large numbers of documents would be a great challenge to the accounts team.

Check out the full blog here

In the world of ERP systems, the integration of centralized customer payments is a game-changer for businesses looking to streamline their operations. As we navigate the complexities of SAP S/4HANA, understanding how to effectively implement centralized payment processes can significantly enhance financial efficiency and accuracy. 

By centralizing customer payments, organizations can reduce manual errors, improve cash flow management, and provide a more cohesive experience for both customers and finance teams. I’ve recently worked on configuring this feature within SAP, focusing on the integration between MM and FI modules, which has proven to be invaluable for our clients. 

For those looking to dive deeper into this topic, I recommend exploring SAP notes related to payment processing and leveraging the SAP CAL for hands-on testing. Sharing resources and insights is crucial as we all aim to build a supportive community of SAP professionals. Let’s continue to learn from each other and drive innovation in our field! #SAP #S4HANA #Finance #ERP #CentralizedPayments #CommunitySupport

actual_review_data.review_text:
Just finished the course “Learning React.js”! #reactjs
Made my first web app UI with React.

Look out for these key concepts:
Creating and rendering React elements,
Representing UI elements with React components,
adding props to display dynamic data,
Building components with JS class,
Using React states to re-render and change state of components,
bind event functions to UI elements with state variables,
and Conditional rendering of components.

JSX is a boon! Makes writing your own components much easier and makes my source much cleaner.

Eve Porcello does a great job of breaking down React concepts, and a hands-on example to help you build on your own.

As technology professionals, we are constantly navigating the evolving landscape of programming frameworks. Recently, I've been diving deep into the integration of AI with frameworks like TensorFlow and React, and I wanted to share some insights that might help others on their journey. 

1. **Start with the Basics**: Before jumping into complex implementations, ensure you have a solid understanding of the foundational concepts. This will make learning new frameworks much more manageable.

2. **Hands-On Practice**: The best way to learn is by doing. Build small projects that incorporate the frameworks you're interested in. This will not only solidify your understanding but also give you practical experience to discuss in interviews.

3. **Leverage Community Resources**: Don’t hesitate to seek out tutorials, forums, and mentorship opportunities. Platforms like GitHub and Stack Overflow are invaluable for troubleshooting and learning from others’ experiences.

4. **Stay Updated**: The tech world moves fast. Follow relevant blogs, podcasts, and communities to keep your knowledge fresh and relevant.

5. **Share Your Journey**: As you learn, share your experiences with your network. Whether it’s a blog post, a LinkedIn update, or a tweet, sharing can foster community engagement and support others who are on a similar path.

Remember, the goal is not just to learn a framework but to understand how it can be applied to solve real-world problems. Let’s continue to support each other in our learning journeys! What frameworks are you currently exploring? 

#Programming #AI #Learning #CommunitySupport #TechIntegration

Wow , Lyft just open sourced its autonomous driving dataset from its Level 5 self-driving fleet!

Awesome stuff, Logan Green & team 

Download their Level 5 dataset: 
___________________________________________________
For reference, the Lyft Level 5 Dataset includes:
1) Over 55,000 human-labeled 3D annotated frames;
2) Data from 7 cameras and up to 3 lidars;
3) A drivable surface map; and,
4) An underlying HD spatial semantic map (including lanes, crosswalks, etc.)
___________________________________________________

initial_prediction_data.review_text:
🚗💡 Exciting times in the world of autonomous vehicles! As we continue to explore the intersection of AI and technology, I wanted to share some invaluable open-source datasets that can significantly enhance your development efforts in this space. 

1. **KITTI Dataset**: A benchmark for many autonomous driving tasks, providing stereo images, 3D point clouds, and more. Perfect for training perception algorithms!

2. **Cityscapes Dataset**: Focused on semantic understanding of urban street scenes, this dataset is essential for segmentation tasks in autonomous driving.

3. **ApolloScape**: Offers a rich set of data for various driving scenarios, including 3D maps and traffic conditions, which can be a game-changer for simulation and testing.

4. **Berkeley DeepDrive**: A comprehensive dataset that includes diverse driving scenarios, helping to improve the robustness of your models.

5. **Waymo Open Dataset**: A large-scale dataset that provides high-resolution sensor data, ideal for training and validating your algorithms.

These resources not only provide a solid foundation for developing autonomous vehicle technologies but also emphasize the importance of collaboration and community support in driving innovation. Let’s leverage these datasets to push the boundaries of what’s possible in autonomous driving! 

Feel free to share your experiences or additional resources in the comments. Together, we can foster a community of learning and growth in this exciting field! 🚀 #AI #AutonomousVehicles #OpenSource #CommunitySupport #Innovation

actual_review_data.review_text:
“The battle for #artificialintelligence supremacy is a binary game between US & China ”— this is what the media likes you to believe. 

My last talk in Berlin debunked exactly this propagated myth. 

I had highlighted that AI innovation can and will come from anywhere since:
 1) talent, 
2)Infrastructure and 
3) AI tech & tools 
are accessible to anyone with internet and hunger to solve a problem. 

Do you agree?
actual_review_data.product_description: Global competition and collaboration in artificial intelligence

initial_prediction_data.review_text:
As we navigate the rapidly evolving landscape of artificial intelligence, it's crucial to recognize the dual forces of global competition and collaboration that shape our industry. 🌍🤝 

In my experience at Microsoft and previous roles, I've seen firsthand how collaboration can lead to groundbreaking innovations. When teams across borders unite their expertise, we not only enhance our technological capabilities but also democratize access to AI solutions that can transform lives. 

However, we also face fierce competition that drives us to push the boundaries of what's possible. This competition can be a powerful motivator, but it’s essential that we balance it with a spirit of collaboration. By sharing knowledge and best practices, we can elevate the entire industry and ensure that AI serves as a force for good. 

I encourage all of us in the tech community to engage in conversations that foster collaboration. Let’s share our insights, mentor the next generation, and work together to harness AI's potential responsibly. 💡✨ 

What are your thoughts on how we can better collaborate in the face of competition? Let’s discuss in the comments! 

#AI #Collaboration #Innovation #Community #Mentorship

actual_review_data.review_text:
Not many of you may know but I spent a lot of my career in the pathology laboratories doing software engineering.

I have often thought how AI will revolutionize patient care, especially in terms of diagnostic services such as radiology and pathology. So it was exciting to see new research from my current employer Microsoft and my former, the UW, rolling research into exactly this, using AI to improve cancer outcomes.

Thinking of the good old days when Rodney Schmidt and I were trying "change the world" with software!

At Microsoft, we are committed to leveraging Azure AI to empower healthcare professionals with tools that not only improve efficiency but also enhance patient care.

With the rise of complex applications and the need for seamless connectivity, our focus has been on enhancing Azure's capabilities to support robust networking solutions that are not only efficient but also secure.

This tool allows for streamlined routing and IP address management, ensuring that our customers can scale their applications without compromising on performance.

Accessibility in tech isn’t just about making tools easier to use; it’s about fostering an inclusive environment where everyone can contribute to innovation. f20ab826-b48b-42cf-be34-de640b4befa1_rev.product_description: Routing management capabilities in cloud environments

actual_review_data.review_text:
Today is a great day for managing your routing at scale in Azure! I'm happy to announce that my team is delivering the capability to manage your user-defined route tables at scale.

One of the complexities customers face with route tables in Azure is the management of linking them to multiple subnets and then managing the routing rules at a larger scale. With route management functionality coming into Azure Virtual Network Manager, customers can now simply create a single definition and apply it to hundreds of subnets with just a few clicks.

Additionally customers have asked for the capability to have more than the 400 rule limit that exists today. Customers who use AVNM for their route management will be able to create up to 1,000 rules in their route tables. This gives our customers the ability to create more complex and detailed route tables.

Great work by my team and I look forward to hearing how this feature helps your organization.

actual_review_data.review_text:
I've been thinking a lot about what customers want to see from Azure Networking, specifically Virtual Networks and software defined networking. The product I work on provides hub/spoke and mesh connectivity between virtual networks, admin rules (think firewall rules) at a virtual network level, and we have route management and NSG management on its way. 

What do you want to see from Azure Networking? What would make your lives easier when working with virtual networks in Azure?

actual_review_data.review_text: Excited to see Virtual Network Verifier (or VNV as we call it) get launched to public preview!! One of the biggest challenges for customers is being able to verify traffic flows within their complex configurations. With VNV customers can now very quickly answer questions like "Why can't my VM reach my database?" Please take a moment to go try it for yourself.
